get.description:
By passing in the appropriate options, you can search for an
available person in the system

parameters.name: rasa-token
parameters.description: send your session token received from the token endpoint

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: pass an optional email for looking up a person

parameters.name: first_name
parameters.description: pass an optional first name for looking up a person

parameters.name: last_name
parameters.description: pass an optional last name for looking up a person

parameters.name: external_id
parameters.description: pass an optional external_id for looking up a person.

parameters.name: is_active
parameters.description: pass an optional is_active for looking up active persons

parameters.name: is_subscribed
parameters.description: pass an optional is_subscribed for looking up subscribed/unsubscribed persons

parameters.name: is_receiving
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return members who are both active and subscribed; pass 0 to return members who are either inactive or unsubscribed

parameters.name: schedule_id
parameters.description: Pass the ID for a schedule to return members receiving on that schedule

parameters.name: updated_since
parameters.description: pass an optional updated_since for looking up persons updated since

parameters.name: updated_before
parameters.description: pass an optional updated_before for looking up persons updated before

parameters.name: created_since
parameters.description: pass an optional created_since for looking up persons created since

parameters.name: created_before
parameters.description: pass an optional created_before for looking up persons created before

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: number of records to skip for pagination

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: maximum number of records to return

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: comma separated list of fields to return

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: order returned records

delete.summary: Deletes a person by id.  This is an Undoable action.  Use with caution.
delete.description: Deletes a person by id - this is an undoable action, and should be reserved for uses such as GDPR deletion.  This is not the action to use for normal de-activation of subscribed members.

parameters.description: send your session token received from the token endpoint

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id that needs to be deleted

parameters.name: rasa-token
parameters.description: send your session token received from the token endpoint

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Filter actions by person ID (GUID format)

parameters.name: person_id
parameters.description: Filter actions by person ID (GUID format)

parameters.name: external_id
parameters.description: Filter actions by external identifier

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Filter actions by email address (supports partial matching)

parameters.name: action_type
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of action types to include. Supported values: 'click', 'open'. Defaults to both if not specified.

parameters.name: issue_date
parameters.description: Filter actions by specific issue date (YYYY-MM-DD format)

parameters.name: created_since
parameters.description: Lower bound timestamp for filtering actions (ISO 8601 format). Defaults to 7 days ago. Cannot be more than billing plan's lookback period (1-24 months).

parameters.name: created_before
parameters.description: Upper bound timestamp for filtering actions (ISO 8601 format). Must be after created_since if both are provided.

parameters.name: exclude_suspect_clicks
parameters.description: Set to 'true' to exclude suspect clicks from results. Defaults to including all clicks.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: Pagination token (base64-encoded) for retrieving next page of results. Use the 'next_link' value from the previous response.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return per page

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return. Available fields: id, person_id, external_id, email, action_type, issue_date, url, created, email_subject, useragent, title, confidence. Use 'all' for all fields.

parameters.name: rasa-token
parameters.description: send your session token received from the token endpoint

get.summary: Execute analytics query for activity data
get.description: Execute analytics queries for activity data. Supports flexible date ranges, segmentation, and aggregation options.

parameters.name: rasa-token
parameters.description: send your session token received from the token endpoint

parameters.name: date_range
parameters.description: Pre-defined date range: 'yesterday', 'today', 'past_week', 'last_week', 'past_month', 'last_month', 'last_three_months', 'last_quarter'. For custom date ranges, use start_date and end_date parameters instead. Defaults to 'past_week'.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date for custom date range in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2024-01-01'). Must be used together with end_date.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date for custom date range in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2024-01-31'). Must be used together with start_date.

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Time bucket interval for aggregating analytics data: 'day', 'week', or 'month'. Defaults to 'day'.

parameters.name: metrics
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of email event metrics to track. Available: 'open', 'click', 'delivered'. Defaults to 'open,click'.

parameters.name: suspect_click
parameters.description: Filter clicks based on bot/automated detection: 'all_clicks', 'real_clicks', or 'suspect_clicks'. Defaults to 'all_clicks'.

parameters.name: segment_code
parameters.description: Filter results by subscriber segment code. Provide a specific segment code, 'All' for all segments, or 'No Code' for subscribers without a segment.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Timezone for date calculations (e.g., 'America/New_York'). Uses community default if not specified.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must be between 1 and 10000. Defaults to 50.

403.description: insufficient permissions - requires premium API access

get.summary: Execute analytics query for article click data
get.description: Execute analytics queries for article click data to identify most clicked articles. Returns articles ranked by click volume with detailed metadata.

parameters.name: rasa-token
parameters.description: send your session token received from the token endpoint

parameters.name: date_range
parameters.description: Pre-defined date range: 'yesterday', 'today', 'past_week', 'last_week', 'past_month', 'last_month', 'last_three_months', 'last_quarter'. For custom date ranges, use start_date and end_date parameters instead. Defaults to 'past_week'.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date for custom date range in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2024-01-01'). Must be used together with end_date.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date for custom date range in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2024-01-31'). Must be used together with start_date.

parameters.name: suspect_click
parameters.description: Filter clicks based on bot/automated detection: 'all_clicks', 'real_clicks', or 'suspect_clicks'. Defaults to 'all_clicks'.

parameters.name: segment_code
parameters.description: Filter results by subscriber segment code. Provide a specific segment code, 'All' for all segments, or 'No Code' for subscribers without a segment.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Timezone for date calculations (e.g., 'America/New_York'). Uses community default if not specified.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of article results to return. Must be between 1 and 10000. Defaults to 50.

403.description: insufficient permissions - requires premium API access

get.summary: Execute analytics query for image click data
get.description: Execute analytics queries for static image click data to identify most clicked images. Returns images ranked by click volume with detailed metadata. Note: This endpoint only tracks click data - metrics parameter is not applicable.

parameters.name: rasa-token
parameters.description: send your session token received from the token endpoint

parameters.name: date_range
parameters.description: Pre-defined date range: 'yesterday', 'today', 'past_week', 'last_week', 'past_month', 'last_month', 'last_three_months', 'last_quarter'. For custom date ranges, use start_date and end_date parameters instead. Defaults to 'past_week'.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date for custom date range in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2024-01-01'). Must be used together with end_date.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date for custom date range in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2024-01-31'). Must be used together with start_date.

parameters.name: suspect_click
parameters.description: Filter clicks based on bot/automated detection: 'all_clicks', 'real_clicks', or 'suspect_clicks'. Defaults to 'all_clicks'.

parameters.name: segment_code
parameters.description: Filter results by subscriber segment code. Provide a specific segment code, 'All' for all segments, or 'No Code' for subscribers without a segment.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Timezone for date calculations (e.g., 'America/New_York'). Uses community default if not specified.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of image results to return. Must be between 1 and 10000. Defaults to 50.

403.description: insufficient permissions - requires premium API access

get.summary: Execute analytics query for topic engagement data
get.description: Execute analytics queries for topic engagement data from the tags index. Returns topics ranked by total interactions with unique user engagement metrics. Note: This endpoint aggregates all topic interactions - metrics parameter is not applicable.

parameters.name: rasa-token
parameters.description: send your session token received from the token endpoint

parameters.name: date_range
parameters.description: Pre-defined date range: 'yesterday', 'today', 'past_week', 'last_week', 'past_month', 'last_month', 'last_three_months', 'last_quarter'. For custom date ranges, use start_date and end_date parameters instead. Defaults to 'past_week'.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date for custom date range in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2024-01-01'). Must be used together with end_date.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date for custom date range in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2024-01-31'). Must be used together with start_date.

parameters.name: suspect_click
parameters.description: Filter clicks based on bot/automated detection: 'all_clicks', 'real_clicks', or 'suspect_clicks'. Defaults to 'all_clicks'.

parameters.name: segment_code
parameters.description: Filter results by subscriber segment code. Provide a specific segment code, 'All' for all segments, or 'No Code' for subscribers without a segment.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Timezone for date calculations (e.g., 'America/New_York'). Uses community default if not specified.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of topic results to return. Must be between 1 and 10000. Defaults to 100.

403.description: insufficient permissions - requires premium API access

next_link.description: URL to access next page of records for original query (if applicable)

record_count.description: number of data records returned, optional
number of data records returned, optional

total_query_count.description: total count of community members matching the submitted filter

end_date.description: UTC end date/time used for query

start_date.description: UTC start date/time used for query

id.description: Returned if the update was successfully processed

error.description: Returned if the update was not successfully processed

status_code.description: Returned if the update was not successfully processed

site_name.example: Optional Site Name of the Post

first_click.description: date of first click on on post with this topic

last_click.description: date of last click on post with this topic

status.description: Message run status (e.g., Delivered, HardBounce, etc.)

oneOf.description: Pre-defined date range: 'yesterday' (previous calendar day), 'today' (current day so far), 'past_week' (last 7 days), 'last_week' (previous Monday-Sunday), 'past_month' (last 30 days), 'last_month' (previous calendar month), 'last_three_months' (previous 3 calendar months), 'last_quarter' (previous calendar quarter)

end_date.description: End date in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2024-01-31'). Must be after start_date.

oneOf.description: Custom date range with specific start and end dates

date_range.description: Date range for analytics query. Can be either a predefined string or a custom range object with start_date and end_date. Defaults to 'past_week' if not specified.

interval.description: Time bucket interval for aggregating analytics data. Use 'day' for daily breakdowns, 'week' for weekly aggregations, or 'month' for monthly summaries. Only applies to /analytics/activities endpoint.

metrics.description: Array of email event metrics to track. Available options: 'opens' (email opens), 'clicks' (link clicks), 'delivered' (successful deliveries), 'bounces' (delivery failures), 'unsubscribes' (opt-outs). Only applies to /analytics/activities endpoint. Defaults to ['opens', 'clicks'] if not specified.

suspect_click.description: Filter clicks based on bot/automated detection. Options: 'all_clicks' (include all clicks regardless of source), 'real_clicks' (exclude suspected bot/automated clicks), 'suspect_clicks' (only show suspected bot/automated clicks). Defaults to 'all_clicks'.

segment_code.description: Filter results by subscriber segment code. Provide a specific segment code to filter to that segment, 'All' to include all segments, or 'No Code' to show only subscribers without a segment assignment.

source_filter.description: Filter content by source type (for content-related analytics)

timezone.description: Timezone for date calculations. Uses community default if not specified

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

query_params.description: Processed query parameters used for the analytics query

timestamp.description: Time period for this data point

unique_opens.description: Number of unique subscribers who opened emails

unique_clicks.description: Number of unique subscribers who clicked links

unique_delivered.description: Number of unique subscribers who received emails

unique_bounces.description: Number of unique subscribers with bounced emails

results.description: Array of articles ranked by click volume

query_params.description: Processed query parameters used for the analytics query

title.example: How to Build Better Email Campaigns

description.example: Learn the best practices for creating effective email marketing campaigns

total_clicks.description: Total number of clicks on this article

unique_clicks.description: Number of unique users who clicked this article

click_rate.description: Click-through rate as percentage (unique clicks / total clicks * 100)

results.description: Array of images ranked by click volume

query_params.description: Processed query parameters used for the analytics query

start_date.description: Campaign start date for the image

end_date.description: Campaign end date for the image

total_clicks.description: Total number of clicks on this image

unique_clicks.description: Number of unique users who clicked this image

click_rate.description: Click-through rate as percentage (unique clicks / total clicks * 100)

results.description: Array of topics ranked by total interactions

query_params.description: Processed query parameters used for the analytics query

total_interactions.description: Total number of interactions with this topic

unique_users.description: Number of unique users who interacted with this topic

engagement_rate.description: Engagement rate as percentage (unique users / total interactions * 100)

BearerAuth.description: Bearer token authentication. Obtain token via POST /tokens
